title: ClickUp Skill Windows UTF-8 Encoding Fix
summary: Fix UnicodeEncodeError on Windows when ClickUp skill scripts output emojis or special characters by adding UTF-8 encoding configuration to the shared common.py module.
keyOutcomes: All ClickUp skill scripts handle Unicode output correctly on Windows
Single-point fix in common.py — no per-script changes needed
Troubleshooting documentation updated for future self-diagnosis

risks.mitigation: Wrapped in try/except; fallback env var documented

risks.risk: Edit to common.py introduces syntax error breaking all scripts
risks.mitigation: Preflight runs first; py_compile check as fallback

risks.risk: Skill root path differs between host apps
risks.mitigation: Fallback search command included in Phase 0

deliverables: Updated common.py with Windows UTF-8 encoding configuration
Updated references/08-troubleshooting.md with encoding issue documentation
Verified smoke test with emoji task name
validationCriteria: create_task.py succeeds with emoji in task name
Troubleshooting doc contains encoding issue section

phases.name: Phase 0: Setup & Preconditions
phases.focus: Verify environment, locate files, confirm Python version

phases.name: Phase 1: Implement Encoding Fix
phases.focus: Add UTF-8 reconfigure block to common.py

phases.name: Phase 2: Smoke Test
phases.focus: Verify fix works with emoji task names

phases.name: Phase 3: Update Documentation
phases.focus: Add encoding issue to troubleshooting reference
